source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - File Management: Editing file content: the ini_file and lineinfile modules

questions.q: To modify an INI file (sections in [] with key=value pairs), which module is easiest?
questions.why: ini_file edits an INI file's section/option/value directly.

questions.q: Which three pieces of information does the ini_file module require?
options: The section, the option name, and the new value
A regexp, a line, and a path
A src, a dest, and a checksum
questions.why: ini_file needs the section, the option, and the new value (plus the dest path).

questions.q: Which collection provides ini_file in the guide's example?
To ensure a single line is present in a file, or to add/modify one line, which module?
questions.why: lineinfile ensures a given line is present or updated in a file.

questions.q: In lineinfile, how is the line to modify located?
questions.why: lineinfile finds the target line using a regexp.

questions.q: Which two lineinfile parameters say which line to match and what it should become?
questions.why: regexp locates the line (e.g. '^SELINUX='); line sets its new content (e.g. 'SELINUX=enforcing').

questions.q: Which collection provides lineinfile in the guide's example?
